title:
Consultations during the open government programme
	 - data.govt.nz
content:
## Consultations during the open government programme
This page will be archived on the 16 April, 2021 . If you need the information on this page after that date, we are happy to provide.
Open Data NZ rans public consultations on topics of interest to digital communities, and released reports about projects or programmes they ran.
You can access the reports and more information on our blog:
- Richard Stirling hangout
- Top 10 datasets (survey)
- NZGOAL – software extension (public consultation)
- International Open Data Charter (public consultation)
- Code for Aotearoa open data fellowships (report)
- Open Data NZ 2016 overview (report)

#### Local and Central Government
The Programme had ongoing relationships with executive level Data Champions in public service agencies, and was establishing relationships amongst wider government, especially Crown Research Institutes and Local Government agencies.
The Programme also actively participated in and guarded the principles of open government data and information in cross-government initiatives such as the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI), Social Sector Data Integration and Analytics, Open Government Partnership, Government's Strategic Information programme, the ICT Strategy, and the Government's response to the New Zealand Data Futures Forum Report.

#### International Engagement
The Programme maintained ongoing relationships with a range of international organisations; government officials in other open government programmes; and open data champions in civil society.
Open Government Partnership Summit 2015, Mexico - ( speech at summit by Minister Louise Upston )
